nodes.label: Zasady pracy nad langlion (phased workflow rules)
nodes.rationale: Work proceeds in phases per docs/plan-implementacji.md; each phase closes with a plan update, DoD review, and explicit user approval before the next phase; phases are never merged without consent.

nodes.label: Brak vendor lock-in (adapter pattern principle)
nodes.rationale: Every external integration (auth, DB, payments, email, storage) must sit behind an internal contract/adapter; the rest of the app never talks to a vendor SDK directly, so swapping a provider is one adapter swap with zero changes to business logic or UI.

nodes.label: MULTI_TENANCY_MODE flag (required/optional/disabled)
nodes.rationale: A purely cosmetic/UI env flag; it never changes the data model - every business row still carries organization_id or account_id, so re-enabling the mode later needs no migration, only UI discovery.

nodes.label: Passkeys (WebAuthn/FIDO2)
nodes.rationale: Phishing-resistant login not dependent on mailbox access, unlike magic link; implemented as another method behind the existing auth adapter contract, not a parallel system.

nodes.norm_label: wymagania ogolne bazy danych (migrations, postgres portability)

nodes.label: Tenant isolation (owner column, RLS as second line of defense)
nodes.rationale: Every business table must carry an indexed owner column (organization_id/account_id) filtered at the data-access layer; row-level security recommended as an independent second line of defense.
nodes.norm_label: tenant isolation (owner column, rls as second line of defense)

nodes.label: Soft delete i retencja (deletedAt flag, retention window)
nodes.rationale: Key entities (user, org, billing data) should soft-delete with a defined retention window and purge process, for GDPR-style deletion requests.
nodes.norm_label: soft delete i retencja (deletedat flag, retention window)

nodes.label: Zastosowania background jobs (email, onboarding, webhooks, AI, cleanup)
nodes.norm_label: zastosowania background jobs (email, onboarding, webhooks, ai, cleanup)

nodes.label: Wymagania background jobs (idempotency, retry/backoff, cron, observability)
nodes.rationale: Jobs must be idempotent where possible, retried with backoff on transient failure, support cron-like recurrence, and expose status/history visibility.
nodes.norm_label: wymagania background jobs (idempotency, retry/backoff, cron, observability)

nodes.label: Zakres integracji AI SDK (streaming, error/limit handling)
nodes.norm_label: zakres integracji ai sdk (streaming, error/limit handling)

nodes.label: Koszty i limity AI wywolan (tied to plan quota)
nodes.norm_label: koszty i limity ai wywolan (tied to plan quota)

nodes.label: Jakosc kodu (TS strict, ESLint/Prettier enforced in CI)
nodes.norm_label: jakosc kodu (ts strict, eslint/prettier enforced in ci)

nodes.label: Wymagania i18n (translation files, locale detection, URL prefix, locale formats)
nodes.norm_label: wymagania i18n (translation files, locale detection, url prefix, locale formats)

nodes.label: Cel reguly AI-assisted development
nodes.rationale: Codebase organized to ease work with AI assistants (Cursor, Claude Code) - a project-structure requirement, not an end-user feature.

nodes.label: Konkretne wymagania AI-assisted dev (predictable dirs, documented reference examples per pattern)
nodes.rationale: Every repeatable pattern (protected endpoint, tenant-isolated entity, etc.) should have a documented reference example in code to point to.
nodes.norm_label: konkretne wymagania ai-assisted dev (predictable dirs, documented reference examples per pattern)

nodes.label: Provider adapter pattern (contract.ts + concrete impl + barrel)
nodes.rationale: Canonical shape for adding a provider adapter: src/lib/adapters/<name>/ with contract.ts interface, one concrete implementation, and an index.ts barrel.
nodes.norm_label: provider adapter pattern (contract.ts + concrete impl + barrel)

nodes.label: Billing adapter (src/lib/adapters/billing/, stripe.ts + none.ts default)
nodes.rationale: Default provider must never throw at construction because the factory runs at module load and would break next build for anyone without that vendor configured.

nodes.label: Jobs adapter / transactional outbox (enqueue writes row, drain executes)
nodes.rationale: enqueue(writer, ...) always writes a row within the caller's transaction; the adapter drains separately (postgres executes, hosted scheduler forwards). Letting a hosted adapter's enqueue call its own SDK directly destroys atomicity because an HTTP call cannot roll back.
nodes.norm_label: jobs adapter / transactional outbox (enqueue writes row, drain executes)

nodes.label: AdminAuthAdapter (superadmin/user role vocabulary, translated at boundary)
nodes.rationale: Better Auth stores the super-admin flag as user.role; the contract exposes plain isSuperAdmin derived at the boundary so UI and engine cannot disagree. adminUserIds must never be set (bypasses permission checks). Reads belong in features/admin/data.ts, not the adapter.
nodes.norm_label: adminauthadapter (superadmin/user role vocabulary, translated at boundary)

nodes.label: Rate-limit adapter (atomic INSERT ... ON CONFLICT counter, fails open)
nodes.rationale: Two racing requests must serialize against each other, not a caller's write, so there is no writer parameter; decide vs decideNext are two separate helpers to avoid off-by-one limits; a throwing store fails open (allowed:true, warn log) so a DB blip does not become a total outage.
nodes.norm_label: rate-limit adapter (atomic insert ... on conflict counter, fails open)

nodes.label: Translate a string pattern (useTranslations/getTranslations, en.json/pl.json compile-checked)
nodes.rationale: MESSAGES is Record<Locale, typeof en>, so a missing pl.json key is a compile error; getTranslator(locale, ns) is used outside a request (cron/email) since request-scoped hooks throw there.
nodes.norm_label: translate a string pattern (usetranslations/gettranslations, en.json/pl.json compile-checked)

nodes.label: Locale routing in src/proxy.ts (next-intl middleware never imported)
nodes.rationale: Two systems cannot both own the response, so auth default-deny and locale prefixing are composed by hand in one file with one documented ordering; localePrefix:always keeps the proxy redirect-only so the guarded path equals the served path.
nodes.norm_label: locale routing in src/proxy.ts (next-intl middleware never imported)

nodes.label: Structured logging pattern (createLogger, no-console ESLint rule)
nodes.rationale: Message is a constant, everything variable is a field; err is reserved for the caught Error so the stack survives; requestLogger seeds request-scoped context, plain createLogger for jobs via AsyncLocalStorage; LOG_FORMAT=json in production for a collector.
nodes.norm_label: structured logging pattern (createlogger, no-console eslint rule)

nodes.label: Add a background job pattern (JobName/JobPayloads, registry, enqueueJob)
nodes.rationale: Pass tx as writer whenever a job accompanies a business write for atomic commit/rollback; handlers must be idempotent (dedupeKey) since the queue is at-least-once; payloads are JSON and zod-parsed on the way out; a no-op is success, not failure.
nodes.norm_label: add a background job pattern (jobname/jobpayloads, registry, enqueuejob)

nodes.label: Add an email template pattern (TemplateName/TemplateProps, enqueueEmail single delivery path)
nodes.rationale: Never call email.send directly; enqueueEmail is the single delivery path keeping retry, suppression, List-Unsubscribe centralized. Templates are plain JSX rendered by @react-email/render for HTML + plain-text.
nodes.norm_label: add an email template pattern (templatename/templateprops, enqueueemail single delivery path)

nodes.label: Add a tenant-isolated entity pattern (indexed owner column, owner-scoped data layer)
nodes.rationale: The two tenant owners are personal_account and organization; membership/invitation carry an indexed organizationId and every read/write is scoped by it in the feature data layer - never query a tenant table without its owner filter.
nodes.norm_label: add a tenant-isolated entity pattern (indexed owner column, owner-scoped data layer)

nodes.label: Audit a state change pattern (AUDIT_ACTIONS, recordAudit(tx, ...), resolveActor)
nodes.rationale: organizationId is a required audit field; actor comes from resolveActor(session) so impersonated actions attribute to the admin; changed(before, after, fields) returns undefined on a no-op so nothing is logged.
nodes.norm_label: audit a state change pattern (audit_actions, recordaudit(tx, ...), resolveactor)

nodes.label: Add a UI primitive pattern (src/components/ui, semantic tokens, cva, Radix wrapping)
nodes.rationale: Style only with semantic tokens, never raw colors; compose classes with cn() so callers can override; interactive/overlay primitives wrap Radix for free focus management and ARIA.
nodes.norm_label: add a ui primitive pattern (src/components/ui, semantic tokens, cva, radix wrapping)

nodes.label: Design token pattern (globals.css, class-based dark mode via next-themes)
nodes.rationale: Tokens are HSL triplets under :root with a .dark override, mapped via @theme inline; never reintroduce prefers-color-scheme in components or hard-code a color.
nodes.norm_label: design token pattern (globals.css, class-based dark mode via next-themes)

nodes.label: Root layout session read / impersonation banner
nodes.rationale: getServerSession() in the root layout opts every page into dynamic rendering; the impersonation banner lives there because it must cover forbidden.tsx, /login and the admin group with no gap.
nodes.norm_label: root layout session read / impersonation banner

nodes.label: Server-rendered (not statically generated) content pages
nodes.rationale: Next 16 removed the per-route PPR opt-in (experimental_ppr gone); only cacheComponents remains as an app-wide escape hatch, a phase of its own. Nothing in spec 9.1 requires SSG - only visibility with JS disabled, asserted by e2e/content-no-js.spec.ts. sitemap.ts/robots.ts/opengraph-image routes are still static route handlers.

nodes.label: Publish content pattern (meta.ts + content.mdx + registry index.ts)
nodes.rationale: The registry key is the slug, directory name and URL at once - no separate slug field to disagree with the others. Registry entries are literal specifiers so content is traced by the bundler; never replace with a dynamic import by slug. Drafts are filtered at source.ts unconditionally.
nodes.norm_label: publish content pattern (meta.ts + content.mdx + registry index.ts)

nodes.label: Add a public route pattern (public-routes.ts, indexable flag, three consumers)
nodes.rationale: One bare-path entry drives proxy.ts, sitemap.ts and robots.ts so reachable and indexed cannot drift apart; a prefix rule on / would turn default-deny into open access.
nodes.norm_label: add a public route pattern (public-routes.ts, indexable flag, three consumers)

nodes.label: Page metadata pattern (pageMetadata(), openGraph replace-not-merge)
nodes.rationale: Metadata segments replace openGraph rather than merging; a page setting only title/description silently inherits the root's og:title, so every page must use pageMetadata() and e2e asserts og:title, not just <title>.

nodes.label: OG image routes are public by construction
nodes.rationale: Next serves generated images at an extension-less path with hash in the query, so the proxy's dot-based skip does not apply; isMetadataImageRoute matches the suffix explicitly.
nodes.norm_label: og image routes are public by construction

nodes.label: Structured data / JsonLd component (escaped < to prevent stored XSS)
nodes.rationale: JSON.stringify would happily emit </script> from a post title, ending the script element; the < escape in <JsonLd> prevents stored XSS via a blog title.
nodes.norm_label: structured data / jsonld component (escaped < to prevent stored xss)

nodes.label: Prose styling pattern (<Prose>, tokens mapped once, no dark:prose-invert)
nodes.rationale: The tokens already flip under .dark, so dark:prose-invert would layer the plugin's own dark palette on top and break it; e2e/content-prose.spec.ts asserts computed colour.
nodes.norm_label: prose styling pattern (<prose>, tokens mapped once, no dark:prose-invert)

nodes.label: Remark/rehype plugin constraint (string name + serializable options only)
nodes.rationale: Turbopack passes plugins to a Rust loader so a JS function cannot cross that boundary - which is why there is no syntax highlighter requiring function options.
nodes.norm_label: remark/rehype plugin constraint (string name + serializable options only)

nodes.label: ConfirmDialog pattern (portaled dialog + form id + confirmForm attribute)
nodes.norm_label: confirmdialog pattern (portaled dialog + form id + confirmform attribute)

nodes.label: Server action feedback pattern (FormMessage for errors, toast for success)
nodes.norm_label: server action feedback pattern (formmessage for errors, toast for success)

nodes.label: Protected endpoint / server action pattern (requireSession() first line)
nodes.norm_label: protected endpoint / server action pattern (requiresession() first line)

nodes.label: Validation layer pattern (zod at trust boundary, parse-then-authorize)
nodes.rationale: Every value crossing the trust boundary passes a zod schema before business logic; a server action argument is treated as an untrusted input since it is reachable by anyone who can POST the same shape; field errors are suppressed for anti-enumeration paths (sign-in, password reset, unsubscribe).
nodes.norm_label: validation layer pattern (zod at trust boundary, parse-then-authorize)

nodes.label: Soft delete + retention pattern (deletedAt, structural access revocation)
nodes.rationale: organization, personal_account and user carry deletedAt; a soft-deleted user cannot sign in and live sessions die on next request. The purge job is deferred - organization.createdByUserId is onDelete:restrict, so hard-deleting a user who created an org fails at the FK.
nodes.norm_label: soft delete + retention pattern (deletedat, structural access revocation)

nodes.label: Take money / checkout pattern (customer mapping before session, webhook entitles)
nodes.rationale: Persisting the customer mapping before creating a checkout session lets the webhook treat an unresolvable customer as not-ours and ignore it. Nothing on the success redirect path grants access; only the webhook entitles. Routes answer with a URL, not a 3xx, since the caller is a client-side fetch.
nodes.norm_label: take money / checkout pattern (customer mapping before session, webhook entitles)

nodes.label: Receive a provider webhook pattern (signature auth, raw body, marker+effect in one tx, watermark)
nodes.rationale: Signature is the authentication so the route must be exempted from the session guard; request.text() reads the raw body since re-serializing invalidates the signature; idempotency marker+effect commit in one transaction; a provider event timestamp watermark guards against out-of-order retries.
nodes.norm_label: receive a provider webhook pattern (signature auth, raw body, marker+effect in one tx, watermark)

nodes.label: Super-admin action pattern (requireSuperAdmin() first line, no super-admin middleware)
nodes.rationale: proxy.ts is edge-safe with no DB access, so the only edge-available alternative (a cookie claim) is invalidated by impersonation swapping the session cookie; audit Rule A/Rule B split by who owns the DB connection; never read the session after a cookie swap.
nodes.norm_label: super-admin action pattern (requiresuperadmin() first line, no super-admin middleware)

nodes.label: Org-scoped RBAC action pattern (requireOrgPermission(slug, permission) first line)
nodes.rationale: Resolves the active org from the URL slug, checks the centralized role-permission map, and calls Next's forbidden() for a real 403; UI gating via hasPermission is cosmetic only; the active tenant is always derived from the /orgs/[slug] URL, never hidden session state.
nodes.norm_label: org-scoped rbac action pattern (requireorgpermission(slug, permission) first line)

nodes.label: CSP third-party origin pattern (env-driven extra src, strict-dynamic, nonce)
nodes.rationale: CSP is default-deny; script-src uses strict-dynamic so host allowlists are ignored by supporting browsers; the CSP header is set only in the proxy (per-request nonce) never duplicated in next.config.ts, since repeated CSP headers intersect.
nodes.norm_label: csp third-party origin pattern (env-driven extra src, strict-dynamic, nonce)

nodes.label: Rate limit tuning pattern (tierFor first-match-wins, report-only mode, forwarded depth)
nodes.rationale: RATE_LIMIT_FORWARDED_DEPTH is security-critical - set wrong, the limiter is bypassable by rotating a header; billing webhook and cron endpoints are exempt because throttling them would be actively harmful; login limit is enforced separately by the server action itself since it never matches an /api rule.
nodes.norm_label: rate limit tuning pattern (tierfor first-match-wins, report-only mode, forwarded depth)

nodes.label: Cosmetic feature flag pattern (MULTI_TENANCY_MODE, orgsEnabled/orgsExposed, notFound not forbidden)
nodes.rationale: Two booleans (orgsEnabled/orgsExposed) instead of a three-way switch per call site; the data model never moves in any mode; a switched-off feature refuses with notFound(), never forbidden(), since a 403 admits the resource exists; enforcement lives at the existing requireOrgAccess chokepoint, not the proxy.
nodes.norm_label: cosmetic feature flag pattern (multi_tenancy_mode, orgsenabled/orgsexposed, notfound not forbidden)

nodes.label: Security headers i Content Security Policy
nodes.norm_label: security headers i content security policy

nodes.label: Walidacja danych jako nazwana warstwa architektoniczna
nodes.norm_label: walidacja danych jako nazwana warstwa architektoniczna

nodes.label: Zgodność z kontraktem adaptera (local/prod przez zmienną env)
nodes.norm_label: zgodnosc z kontraktem adaptera (local/prod przez zmienna env)

nodes.label: Agent AI przechodzi ten sam RBAC co użytkownik
nodes.norm_label: agent ai przechodzi ten sam rbac co uzytkownik

nodes.label: Audit trail z typem wykonawcy AIAgent
nodes.norm_label: audit trail z typem wykonawcy aiagent

nodes.label: Sekcja 27 - Backup i przywracanie danych per organizacja
nodes.norm_label: sekcja 27 - backup i przywracanie danych per organizacja

nodes.label: Rekomendacja Postgres Row-Level Security per organization_id
nodes.norm_label: rekomendacja postgres row-level security per organization_id

nodes.label: Constraint 1 - brak nakładających się sesji trenera (EXCLUDE gist)
nodes.norm_label: constraint 1 - brak nakładajacych sie sesji trenera (exclude gist)

nodes.label: EPIK 21 - Reasygnacja: trenerzy, sesje, dezaktywacja Definicji
nodes.norm_label: epik 21 - reasygnacja: trenerzy, sesje, dezaktywacja definicji

nodes.label: EPIK 23 - Tryby zakupu i rozliczenia per typ grupy
nodes.norm_label: epik 23 - tryby zakupu i rozliczenia per typ grupy

nodes.label: Ręczny proces fakturowania (invoice_requested_at/invoice_issued_at)
nodes.rationale: Brak automatycznego fakturowania nie może blokować standardowej sprzedaży online — faktura jest procesem ręcznym, opcjonalnym, poza główną ścieżką płatności.

nodes.label: Wersjonowanie dokumentów bez retroaktywności
nodes.rationale: Edycja treści regulaminu tworzy nową wersję; istniejące akceptacje pozostają zamrożone na starej wersji, aby chronić dowody prawne w sporach.

nodes.label: Epic 29 - Limity Planu i Feature Gating
nodes.norm_label: epic 29 - limity planu i feature gating

nodes.label: Fail-closed feature/limit gating
nodes.rationale: Brak zdefiniowanego limit_key blokuje operację (fail-closed); downgrade nigdy nie usuwa danych automatycznie — tylko blokuje dalsze dodawanie.

nodes.label: Epic 30 - Stripe Connect i konfiguracja płatności per organizacja
nodes.norm_label: epic 30 - stripe connect i konfiguracja płatnosci per organizacja

nodes.label: Rozdzielenie kont Stripe: platforma vs Connected Account
nodes.rationale: Płatność za plan platformy (platform_stripe_customer_id) i płatności klientów akademii (stripe_connect_account_id) nigdy się nie mieszają — system jawnie wskazuje cel każdej operacji i webhooka.
nodes.norm_label: rozdzielenie kont stripe: platforma vs connected account

nodes.label: Obecność niezależna od płatności
nodes.rationale: Oznaczanie present/absent nie zmienia payment_status i nie wywołuje żadnych automatycznych konsekwencji (kredyty, powiadomienia) — czysta funkcja informacyjna, oddzielona od rozliczeń.

nodes.label: Moduł wynagrodzeń bez skutków finansowych
nodes.rationale: Raport wynagrodzeń jest wyłącznie informacyjny — nie tworzy płatności, wypłaty ani transferu na żadnym z dwóch kont Stripe; wypłata odbywa się poza systemem.

nodes.label: Nieretroaktywność stawki (Constraint 8)
nodes.rationale: Podniesienie stawki tworzy nowy rekord trainer_rate z effective_from — minione okresy rozliczane po starej stawce, zapewniając powtarzalność już wygenerowanych raportów.

nodes.label: Synchronizacja unit_amount subskrypcji przy zmianie rabatu (Constraint 10)
nodes.rationale: Zmiana ceny bazowej lub aktywnego rabatu procentowego natychmiast synchronizuje subscription_item przez price_data, bez czekania na najbliższe odnowienie; równoległe zadania serializowane sekwencyjnie.
nodes.norm_label: synchronizacja unit_amount subskrypcji przy zmianie rabatu (constraint 10)

nodes.label: Rabat nigdy nie jest samoobsługowy
nodes.rationale: Nie istnieje ścieżka UI/API pozwalająca klientowi samodzielnie zgłosić lub zastosować rabat — decyzja zawsze pochodzi od admina z wymaganym powodem (reason).

nodes.label: Dostępność nigdy nie jest źródłem prawdy o zajętości
nodes.rationale: Brak zdefiniowanego okna nie blokuje sprzedaży (fail-safe); jedynym twardym blokerem zapisu jest realna kolizja constraintu §5.1, nigdy sama dostępność.
nodes.norm_label: dostepnosc nigdy nie jest zrodłem prawdy o zajetosci

nodes.label: Epic 35 - E-dziennik: oceny i notatki o postępach
nodes.norm_label: epic 35 - e-dziennik: oceny i notatki o postepach

nodes.label: Oceny/notatki niezależne od płatności i obecności
nodes.rationale: Wpisywanie ocen/notatek nie zmienia payment_status ani attendance_status i vice versa — te trzy warstwy sesji są celowo rozdzielone.
nodes.norm_label: oceny/notatki niezalezne od płatnosci i obecnosci

nodes.label: Epic 37 - Profil uczestnika: dane zdrowotne, kontakt awaryjny, zgody
nodes.norm_label: epic 37 - profil uczestnika: dane zdrowotne, kontakt awaryjny, zgody

nodes.label: Fail-closed authorization (overlay na statycznej roli)
nodes.rationale: Brak wpisu nigdy nie oznacza 'wolno'; override jest nakładką na statyczną mapę ról, nie jej zamiennikiem, i wymaga uzasadnienia (reason) analogicznie do credits.manual_grant.

nodes.label: Walidacja wierszowa (nie atomowa)
nodes.rationale: Jeden błędny wiersz nie przerywa całej migracji — poprawne wiersze są zapisywane, błędne trafiają na raport błędów (Rozstrzygnięcie #29).

nodes.label: Import nie fabrykuje zgód/akceptacji
nodes.rationale: Import athlete nie tworzy athlete_consent ani policy_acceptance — zgody muszą być realnie wyrażone, nigdy domniemane przy migracji danych.

nodes.label: Epic 40 - Zapis wielu uczestników w jednym przejściu
nodes.norm_label: epic 40 - zapis wielu uczestnikow w jednym przejsciu

nodes.label: Wzorzec częściowego sukcesu (Constraint 15)
nodes.rationale: Każde dziecko to osobna transakcja zajęcia miejsca; niepowodzenie jednego nie wycofuje sukcesu rodzeństwa, a każdy przechodzi pełną ochronę §5 niezależnie.

nodes.label: Brak ścieżki zwrotu przez Stripe dla anulowanej opłaty
nodes.rationale: Anulowanie extra_fee ustawia status=cancelled bez wywołania Stripe Refund — faktyczny zwrot pieniędzy odbywa się poza systemem, analogicznie do ręcznego fakturowania.
nodes.norm_label: brak sciezki zwrotu przez stripe dla anulowanej opłaty

nodes.label: Epic 43 - Tematy lekcji i śledzenie prac domowych
nodes.norm_label: epic 43 - tematy lekcji i sledzenie prac domowych

nodes.label: Epic 44 - Hasło klienta jako alternatywna metoda logowania
nodes.norm_label: epic 44 - hasło klienta jako alternatywna metoda logowania

nodes.label: Reset hasła przez OTP (wymuszony)
nodes.rationale: OTP w resecie hasła nigdy nie jest równoległym fallbackiem logowania — po weryfikacji kodu klient jest wymuszony do ustawienia nowego hasła, a stare sesje są unieważniane w tej samej transakcji.
